{{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Kose Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}}
{{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}
{{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}
{{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}}
{{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}
{{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka}}
{{ill|Jōen-in|de|Jōen-in|ja|浄円院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-in|1=Jōen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6992748}} ? |後裔 = {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family}} subordinate {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}} 2 houses ({{ill|Buke|en|Buke|WD=Q27974264|lt=Buke}}) }} '''Kose Clan''' (巨勢氏) is a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan}} that adopts “Kose” as its clan name. It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose City}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=eight kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th Century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Kose Otoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Kose Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Kose Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|WD=Q14126428|lt=Daihu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose Eji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Kose Otoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Minister}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}. * In Year 2 of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} ({{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Kose Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} participated in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Teimei Rebellion}} and annihilated {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe Moriya}}. * In Year 5 of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika Era}} ({{ill|649|en|649|WD=Q31117|lt=649 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} became {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} sided with the Omi faction in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} and was sentenced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|WD=Q188863|lt=Exile}}. * In Year 13 of Emperor Tenmu ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Kuromaro|de|Kose Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|lt=Kuromaro|lt_ja=黒麻呂|lt_de=Kuromaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kuromaro|1=Kose no Kuromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287613}} and {{ill|Kose Shitan|de|Kose Shitan|ja|巨勢紫檀|lt=Shitan|lt_ja=紫檀|lt_de=Shitan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shitan|1=Kose Shitan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932551}} were granted the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō}} ({{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was conferred the Ranks of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|de|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin}} ({{ill|810|en|810|WD=Q23819|lt=810 AD}}) – {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}} was reappointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}. * {{ill|Kose Chūzen|de|Kose Chūzen|ja|巨勢忠善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Chūzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family}}, and when {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune}} became shogun, they became {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}}. Subsequently, {{ill|Kose Shishin|de|Kose Shishin|ja|巨勢至信|lt=Shishin|lt_ja=至信|lt_de=Shishin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shishin|1=Kose Yukinobu|comment=changed title based on [[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] ]] ) --> {{translated page|ja|巨勢氏|version=90698167|comment=Imported full ja history}} |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Branch|de|Imperial Branch|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Branch|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} |本貫 = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |人物 = {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}
{{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}}
{{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}
{{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}
{{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}}
{{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}
{{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}
{{ill|Jōen-in|de|Jōen-in|ja|浄円院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-in|1=Jōen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6992748}} ? |後裔 = {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} subordinate {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} 2 houses ({{ill|Buke|en|Buke|WD=Q27974264|lt=Buke|1=Buke}}) }} '''Kose Clan''' (巨勢氏) is a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} that adopts “Kose” as its clan name. It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose City|1=Gose, Nara}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th Century|1=6th century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|WD=Q14126428|lt=Daihu|1=Taifu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Minister|1=Minister (government)}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * In Year 2 of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} ({{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587 AD|1=587}}) – {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} participated in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Teimei Rebellion|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} and annihilated {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. * In Year 5 of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika Era|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|649|en|649|WD=Q31117|lt=649 AD|1=649}}) – {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} became {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672 AD|1=672}}) – {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} sided with the Omi faction in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} and was sentenced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|WD=Q188863|lt=Exile|1=Exile}}. * In Year 13 of Emperor Tenmu ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}) – {{ill|Kose Kuromaro|de|Kose Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|lt=Kuromaro|lt_ja=黒麻呂|lt_de=Kuromaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kuromaro|1=Kose no Kuromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287613}} and {{ill|Kose Shitan|de|Kose Shitan|ja|巨勢紫檀|lt=Shitan|lt_ja=紫檀|lt_de=Shitan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shitan|1=Shitan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932551}} were granted the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} ({{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749 AD|1=749}}) – {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was conferred the Ranks of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|de|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} ({{ill|810|en|810|WD=Q23819|lt=810 AD|1=810}}) – {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}} was reappointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}. * {{ill|Kose Chūzen|de|Kose Chūzen|ja|巨勢忠善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Chūzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, and when {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}} became shogun, they became {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}}. Subsequently, {{ill|Kose Shishin|de|Kose Shishin|ja|巨勢至信|lt=Shishin|lt_ja=至信|lt_de=Shishin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shishin|1=Kose Yukinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873474}} and {{ill|Kose Shichū|de|Kose Shichū|ja|巨勢至忠|lt=Shichū|lt_ja=至忠|lt_de=Shichū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shichū|1=Kose Yukitada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873642}} (and so on) followed. == Genealogy == {{familytree/start|style=font-size:80%}} {{familytree |border=0| 001 |001={{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}}} {{familytree |border=0| |!|}} {{familytree |border=0| 001 |001='''Kose'''{{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|lt=Komagari Sukune|lt_ja=小柄宿禰|lt_de=Komagari Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}}} {{familytree |border=0| |)|-|-|v|-|-|.}} {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 |001=Hoshikawa Tatemichi|002=Ito|003=Kori}} {{familytree |border=0| |:| | |:| | |!}} {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 |001=〔{{ill|Jakube Clan|de|Jakube Clan|ja|雀部氏|lt=Jakube Minister|lt_ja=雀部臣|lt_de=Jakube Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakube Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link revision.text:
[[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] ]] ) --> {{translated page|ja|巨勢氏|version=90698167|comment=Imported full ja history}} |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Branch|de|Imperial Branch|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Branch|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} |本貫 = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |人物 = {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}
{{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}}
{{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}
{{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}
{{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}}
{{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}
{{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}
{{ill|Jōen-in|de|Jōen-in|ja|浄円院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-in|1=Jōen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6992748}} ? |後裔 = {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} subordinate {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} 2 houses ({{ill|Buke|en|Buke|WD=Q27974264|lt=Buke|1=Buke}}) }} '''Kose Clan''' (巨勢氏) is a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} that adopts “Kose” as its clan name. It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose City|1=Gose, Nara}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th Century|1=6th century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|WD=Q14126428|lt=Daihu|1=Taifu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Minister|1=Minister (government)}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose City|1=Gose, Nara}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th Century|1=6th century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|WD=Q14126428|lt=Daihu|1=Taifu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Minister|1=Minister (government)}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * In Year 2 of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} ({{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587 AD|1=587}}) – {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} participated in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Teimei Rebellion|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} and annihilated {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. * In Year 5 of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika Era|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|649|en|649|WD=Q31117|lt=649 AD|1=649}}) – {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} became {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672 AD|1=672}}) – {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} sided with the Omi faction in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} and was sentenced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|WD=Q188863|lt=Exile|1=Exile}}. * In Year 13 of Emperor Tenmu ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}) – {{ill|Kose Kuromaro|de|Kose Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|lt=Kuromaro|lt_ja=黒麻呂|lt_de=Kuromaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kuromaro|1=Kose no Kuromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287613}} and {{ill|Kose Shitan|de|Kose Shitan|ja|巨勢紫檀|lt=Shitan|lt_ja=紫檀|lt_de=Shitan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shitan|1=Shitan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932551}} were granted the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} ({{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749 AD|1=749}}) – {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was conferred the Ranks of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|de|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} ({{ill|810|en|810|WD=Q23819|lt=810 AD|1=810}}) – {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}} was reappointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}. * {{ill|Kose Chūzen|de|Kose Chūzen|ja|巨勢忠善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Chūzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, and when {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}} became shogun, they became {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}}. [[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] [[ja:巨勢氏]] ]] ) --> {{translated page|ja|巨勢氏|version=90698167|comment=Imported full ja history}} |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Branch|de|Imperial Branch|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Branch|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} |本貫 = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}} |人物 = {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}
{{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}}
{{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}
{{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}
{{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}}
{{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}
{{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}
{{ill|Jōen-in|de|Jōen-in|ja|浄円院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-in|1=Jōen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6992748}} ? |後裔 = {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} subordinate {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} 2 houses ({{ill|Buke|en|Buke|WD=Q27974264|lt=Buke|1=Buke}}) }} '''Kose Clan''' (巨勢氏) is a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} that adopts “Kose” as its clan name. It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose City|1=Gose, Nara}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th Century|1=6th century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|WD=Q14126428|lt=Daihu|1=Taifu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|WD=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Minister|1=Minister (government)}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * In Year 2 of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} ({{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587 AD|1=587}}) – {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96715944}} participated in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Teimei Rebellion|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} and annihilated {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. * In Year 5 of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika Era|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|649|en|649|WD=Q31117|lt=649 AD|1=649}}) – {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} became {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672 AD|1=672}}) – {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} sided with the Omi faction in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} and was sentenced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|WD=Q188863|lt=Exile|1=Exile}}. * In Year 13 of Emperor Tenmu ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}) – {{ill|Kose Kuromaro|de|Kose Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|lt=Kuromaro|lt_ja=黒麻呂|lt_de=Kuromaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kuromaro|1=Kose no Kuromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287613}} and {{ill|Kose Shitan|de|Kose Shitan|ja|巨勢紫檀|lt=Shitan|lt_ja=紫檀|lt_de=Shitan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shitan|1=Shitan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932551}} were granted the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} ({{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749 AD|1=749}}) – {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479771}} was conferred the Ranks of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|de|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} ({{ill|810|en|810|WD=Q23819|lt=810 AD|1=810}}) – {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479784}} was reappointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}. * {{ill|Kose Chūzen|de|Kose Chūzen|ja|巨勢忠善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Chūzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, and when {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}} became shogun, they became {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}}. Subsequently, {{ill|Kose Shishin|de|Kose Shishin|ja|巨勢至信|lt=Shishin|lt_ja=至信|lt_de=Shishin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shishin|1=Kose Yukinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873474}} and {{ill|Kose Shichū|de|Kose Shichū|ja|巨勢至忠|lt=Shichū|lt_ja=至忠|lt_de=Shichū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shichū|1=Kose Yukitada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873642}} (and so on) followed. == Genealogy == {{familytree/start|style=font-size:80%}} {{familytree |border=0| 001 |001={{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96715944}}
{{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479774}}
{{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479766}}
{{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479771}}
{{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479784}}
{{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|qid=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}
{{ill|Jōen-in|de|Jōen-in|ja|浄円院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-in|1=Jōen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6992748}} ? |後裔 = {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|qid=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} subordinate {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|qid=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} 2 houses ({{ill|Buke|en|Buke|qid=Q27974264|lt=Buke|1=Buke}}) }} '''Kose Clan''' (巨勢氏) is a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|qid=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} that adopts “Kose” as its clan name. It was an {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|qid=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|qid=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clan|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|qid=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}Kose District (巨勢郷) (present‐day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose City|1=Gose, Nara}} Furuse (古瀬)){{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The orthography of “Kose” is attested as '''Kose''' (許勢), , '''Kose''' (居勢), and {{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally read as {{reading|{{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}|o-mi}}, and in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|684|en|684|qid=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Family Names|de|Acht Familiennamen|ja|八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Family Names|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391729}}, it was changed to the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. == Overview == According to traditions such as the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|qid=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}}, the clan is said to descend from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kogen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with its founder being {{ill|Kose Komagari Sukune|de|Kose Komagari Sukune|ja|許勢小柄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Komagari Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 許勢小柄|1=Kose-no-Ogara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18226524}}, the second son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2310}}. After the 6th Century {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th Century|1=6th century}}, the clan emerged as a rising new power through its diplomatic and military engagements with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}. Beginning with {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479782}}, who became a {{ill|Minister (Ancient Japan)|de|Minister (Altes Japan)|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|lt_ja=大臣|lt_de=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister (Ancient Japan)|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4328809}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, followed by {{ill|Kose Inamochi|de|Kose Inamochi|ja|許勢稲持|lt=Inamochi|lt_ja=稲持|lt_de=Inamochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Inamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢稲持|1=Inamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q90866601}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, {{ill|Kose Saru|de|Kose Saru|ja|巨勢猿|lt=Saru|lt_ja=猿|lt_de=Saru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Saru|1=Kose no Saru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q66480252}} and {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96715944}} in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, and {{ill|Kose Daimaro|de|Kose Daimaro|ja|許勢大摩呂|lt=Daimaro|lt_ja=大摩呂|lt_de=Daimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Daimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, members of the clan appear as officials of the {{ill|Taifu|en|Taifu|qid=Q14126428|lt=Daihu|1=Taifu}} (councilor) class{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}, though their genealogical relationships remain unclear. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|qid=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479774}} served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479766}} served as {{ill|Censorial Official|de|Zensor|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Censorial Official|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071155}}; moreover, in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Kose Maro|de|Kose Maro|ja|巨勢麻呂|lt=Maro|lt_ja=麻呂|lt_de=Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Maro|1=Kose no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479786}} and {{ill|Kose Eji|de|Kose Eji|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Eji|lt_ja=邑治|lt_de=Eji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Eji|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479785}} held the position of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, while {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479771}} was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2311}}. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, after producing {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479784}}, who was first appointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}} and later promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, no other member rose to the level of court nobility. {{ill|Kose Kanaoka|en|Kose Kanaoka|qid=Q3076707|lt=Kanaoka|1=Kose Kanaoka}}, the founder of the {{ill|Kose School|de|Kose Schule|ja|巨勢派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose School|1=Kose school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479780}}, was a great-grandson of Noashi. == Developments == * Early 6th Century – In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, {{ill|Kose Otoko|de|Kose Otoko|ja|巨勢男人|lt=Man|lt_ja=男人|lt_de=Man|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Otoko|1=Man|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479782}} became a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Minister|1=Minister (government)}}, and his daughters {{ill|Sadehime|de|Sadehime|ja|紗手媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kakayuhime|de|Kakayuhime|ja|香々有媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakayuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were married to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|qid=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * In Year 2 of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|qid=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} ({{ill|587|en|587|qid=Q30891|lt=587 AD|1=587}}) – {{ill|Kose Hirao|de|Kose Hirao|ja|巨勢比良夫|lt=Hirao|lt_ja=比良夫|lt_de=Hirao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hirao|1=Hirao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96715944}} participated in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|qid=Q534720|lt=Teimei Rebellion|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} and annihilated {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|qid=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. * In Year 5 of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika Era|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|649|en|649|qid=Q31117|lt=649 AD|1=649}}) – {{ill|Kose Tokuda|de|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳陀|lt=Tokuda|lt_ja=徳陀|lt_de=Tokuda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巨勢徳多|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479774}} became {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672 AD|1=672}}) – {{ill|Kose Hito|de|Kose Hito|ja|巨勢人|lt=Hito|lt_ja=人|lt_de=Hito|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479766}} sided with the Omi faction in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} and was sentenced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|qid=Q188863|lt=Exile|1=Exile}}. * In Year 13 of Emperor Tenmu ({{ill|684|en|684|qid=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}) – {{ill|Kose Kuromaro|de|Kose Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|lt=Kuromaro|lt_ja=黒麻呂|lt_de=Kuromaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kuromaro|1=Kose no Kuromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109287613}} and {{ill|Kose Shitan|de|Kose Shitan|ja|巨勢紫檀|lt=Shitan|lt_ja=紫檀|lt_de=Shitan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shitan|1=Shitan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30932551}} were granted the surname {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|qid=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} ({{ill|749|en|749|qid=Q23624|lt=749 AD|1=749}}) – {{ill|Kose Neta Maro|de|Kose Neta Maro|ja|巨勢奈弖麻呂|lt=Neta Maro|lt_ja=奈弖麻呂|lt_de=Neta Maro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Neta Maro|1=Kose no Natemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479771}} was conferred the Ranks of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|de|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. * In Year 1 of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} ({{ill|810|en|810|qid=Q23819|lt=810 AD|1=810}}) – {{ill|Kose Noashi|de|Kose Noashi|ja|巨勢野足|lt=Noashi|lt_ja=野足|lt_de=Noashi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Noashi|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479784}} was reappointed as {{ill|Kurando-tō|de|Kurando-tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurando-tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}}. * {{ill|Kose Chūzen|de|Kose Chūzen|ja|巨勢忠善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Chūzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|qid=Q11605046|lt=Kishu Tokugawa Family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, and when {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|qid=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}} became shogun, they became {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|qid=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}}. Subsequently, {{ill|Kose Shishin|de|Kose Shishin|ja|巨勢至信|lt=Shishin|lt_ja=至信|lt_de=Shishin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shishin|1=Kose Yukinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24873474}} and {{ill|Kose Shichū|de|Kose Shichū|ja|巨勢至忠|lt=Shichū|lt_ja=至忠|lt_de=Shichū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Shichū|1=Kose Yukitada|comment=changed title based on
